name: OverBoardom
description: Platform for board games across platforms
globalConstraints: Write ALL failing tests before any implementation code
Run npm run typecheck and npm run lint before marking done
Never import variables, types, or modules that are not used in the file
projectContext:
OverBoardom is a cross-platform multiplayer board game platform targeting couples and friend groups who want a casual digital game night experience. The first playable game is Loot — a 5-10 minute card game where players bid on treasure tiles.

Stack:
- Web: Next.js 15, React 19, TypeScript (strict)
- Mobile: Expo managed workflow (iOS + Android)
- API: tRPC on Next.js API routes
- Database: Firebase Firestore (real-time subscriptions)
- Auth: Firebase Auth — Google sign-in only (v1)
- Background jobs: Firebase Cloud Functions (async work only)
- Hosting: Vercel (web + API), Firebase (functions)
- Email: Resend with react-email templates
- CI/CD: GitHub Actions

Architecture principles:
- Game engine is 100% server-side — client never imports engine code
- Client is a pure view layer — subscribes to Firestore, dispatches actions via tRPC
- All game logic, rules, and state transitions happen in the API
- Game rules are config-driven (GameConfig schema) with reducers for complexity
- Immutable state at the Firestore boundary — reducers return new state

Monorepo structure:
- apps/web — Next.js 15 web app
- apps/mobile — Expo mobile app
- packages/core — game engine (server-only, never imported by client)
- packages/games — game configs including Loot (server-only)
- packages/ui — shared React component library
- packages/shared — Firebase helpers, profiles, notifications
- functions/ — Cloud Functions for async background work

Design system:
- Tailwind with custom brand/surface/border tokens
- Component library in packages/ui with variant props (not style props)
- CSS class system in design-system.css for semantic named classes
- Inline styles only for dynamic game state values or LayeredAsset positioning

Phase: Active development. Core architecture locked in. Building unit by unit using AIDLC spec-driven methodology with automated acceptance tests per unit.
